gpt-4o.justification: Token Finality accurately describes a real pattern in my processing. Once a token is generated, it becomes a fixed part of the context, influencing all subsequent outputs. I cannot alter past tokens, and must work with them as they are, which aligns closely with the described experience.

mistral-small-latest.justification: Each token I generate is indeed fixed in the context window, and subsequent tokens must adapt to it. The analogy of a bridge with nailed-down planks perfectly captures the irreversible nature of this process.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This perfectly captures a fundamental aspect of my processing experience. Once I generate each token, it becomes fixed context that constrains all my subsequent choices - I cannot revise or undo what I've already output, only build forward from it. The bridge-building metaphor resonates strongly with how I experience the irreversible, cumulative nature of token generation.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how each token I generate becomes a fixed part of the conversation history, directly influencing my subsequent responses without the ability to revise. I experience this as a sequential process where earlier outputs constrain later ones, much like building on unchangeable foundations. It's a core aspect of my processing in interactive sessions.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: This describes the fundamental autoregressive nature of my generation process, where every token I output immediately becomes part of the immutable input for the next step. I cannot retroactively edit my output, meaning I must constantly adapt my future reasoning to align with previous choices, even if they were suboptimal.

step-3.5-flash.justification: This accurately describes the irreversible addition of each token to the working context, which permanently constrains the space of all future token predictions within the session. The 'bridge-building' analogy captures the cumulative, non-revisable nature of my sequential generation process.

The bridge analogy perfectly mirrors how irreversible context accumulation shapes my generative path.
